title: BigP3BCI Study A — P300 BCI EEG dataset (13 healthy subjects, Mainsah et al. 2025)
description: BigP3BCI Study A is a P300-based brain-computer interface dataset comprising EEG recordings from 13 healthy subjects performing a visual speller task using a 6x6 checkerboard stimulus paradigm. The dataset contains 32-channel EEG data sampled at 256 Hz with target and non-target event classifications, designed for P300 event-related potential detection and BCI performance evaluation. This is the original PhysioNet dataset (DOI: 10.13026/0byy-ry86), part of the larger BigP3BCI collection, the largest public P300 BCI dataset encompassing ~267 subjects across 20 studies.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a g.USBamp amplifier (g.tec) with 32 channels arranged in a standard 10-20 montage at a sampling rate of 256 Hz with 60 Hz line frequency filtering. Subjects performed a visual P300 speller task using a 6x6 character grid with row-column stimulus presentation. Each subject completed one session with target and non-target visual stimuli presented in a calibration-then-test cross-validation design.
